llm_result.summary: The case reflects a high level of acute bodily symptom intensity with strong threat appraisal, avoidance of risky contexts, and interoceptive hypervigilance, despite normal medical test results. Modifiable levers should target (1) the brain-body threat conditioning and attention to internal signals (BIO+PSYCHO), (2) avoidance behaviors and real-world functioning (SOCIAL), and (3) interpretation of symptoms and response to reassurance (PSYCHO). An integrated plan that uses graded exposure, cognitive-behavioral strategies, acceptance-based approaches, autonomic regulation, and supportive social/health-system strategies is likely to reduce symptom frequency/severity, distress from unpredictability, and avoidance, while improving daily functioning and quality of life.

solutions.predictor_variable: Interoceptive Exposure + Mindfulness Training for Bodily Sensations
solutions.solution_candidate: Systematic interoceptive exposure to feared bodily sensations paired with mindfulness-based attention retraining to reduce catastrophic interpretation and hypervigilance. Components include in-session graded exposures to sensations (e.g., mild GI sensations, heartbeat focus, dizziness simulations) with daily self-guided audio and brief weekly psychoeducation to normalize bodily signals.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reducing fear-conditioned responses to internal cues lowers autonomic arousal, decreases threat amplification, and reduces both distress from unpredictability and excessive interoceptive focus.
solutions.measurement: Daily symptom diary (frequency and 0–10 peak intensity); interoceptive attention scale (0–5); weekly log of completed exposures; optional resting HRV during tasks.
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor for acute distress during early exposures; stop or modify if panic symptoms worsen; ensure access to clinician if symptoms escalate.
solutions.mapping_query: Map S01 to C01, C02, C03, and C06; track changes via daily symptom diaries and exposure logs.

solutions.predictor_variable: Graded Real-World Exposure to Leaving Home/Travel
solutions.solution_candidate: Structured, progressive exposure program to reduce travel-avoidance and improve confidence away from home. Start with short, safe excursions near home and gradually increase distance/time in a stepwise ladder, with contingency planning for symptoms and a support person or coach.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Systematic exposure to avoided contexts reduces avoidance behavior, lowers anticipatory anxiety, and improves daily functioning and resilience in real-world settings.
solutions.measurement: Weekly distance from home (meters/kilometers) and time spent away; avoidance days; self-reported fear during excursions (0–10).
solutions.safety_notes: Proceed with safety plan; pause or slow the ladder if panic or severe symptom escalation occurs; ensure support contact during initial travels.
solutions.mapping_query: Map S02 to C05 and C02/C07; monitor travel distance and avoidance reduction over time.

solutions.solution_candidate: Cognitive-behavioral interventions to challenge catastrophic interpretations of bodily sensations and reduce perceived danger. Includes cognitive restructuring, behavioral experiments to test feared outcomes, and relapse-prevention planning.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Altering automatic threat appraisals lowers fear responses to sensations and increases trust in benign medical information, reducing distress when symptoms arise.
solutions.measurement: Threat appraisal rating (0–10) before/after sessions; frequency of catastrophic thoughts; satisfaction with medical reassurance.
solutions.safety_notes: Ensure collaboration with healthcare providers; monitor for misinterpretations that require clinician review.
solutions.mapping_query: Map S03 to C03 and C04; track threat perception changes and reassurance acceptance.

solutions.solution_candidate: Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT)–informed practices to increase tolerance of uncertainty and encourage value-consistent actions despite symptoms. Includes values clarification, committed action plans, and experiential exercises.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Increasing acceptance of uncertainty reduces distress from unpredictable symptoms and supports engagement in meaningful activities, lowering functional impairment.
solutions.measurement: Acceptance of uncertainty scale; values-aligned activities completed per week; distress and engagement scores.
solutions.safety_notes: No safety concerns; ensure alignment with medical advice and patient preferences.
solutions.mapping_query: Map S04 to C02 and C07; monitor changes in acceptance and activity engagement.

solutions.predictor_variable: Autonomic Regulation via HRV Biofeedback & Paced Breathing
solutions.solution_candidate: Physiological regulation program using HRV biofeedback and paced breathing to reduce autonomic arousal, improve emotional regulation, and lessen symptom intensity.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Improved autonomic balance dampens excessive bodily arousal and lowers interoceptive threat sensitivity.
solutions.measurement: HRV metrics (RMSSD, HF power); resting heart rate; episode intensity and frequency logs; breathing rate during practice.
solutions.safety_notes: Occasional dizziness during training should be monitored; adapt practice as needed; seek clinician input if uncomfortable changes occur.
solutions.mapping_query: Map S05 to C01, C02, C06; use physiological data to gauge arousal changes.

solutions.solution_candidate: Sleep hygiene and circadian regularity program (consistent sleep/wake times, light exposure, wind-down routines) to stabilize physiological systems and improve symptom regulation.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Better sleep supports autonomic regulation and mood, reducing symptom frequency/severity and emotional interference.
solutions.measurement: Sleep duration/consistency (actigraphy or diary); Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index scores; daytime fatigue/functional impact reports.
solutions.safety_notes: Minimal risk; monitor for sleep disturbances during routine changes; adjust as needed.
solutions.mapping_query: Map S06 to C01 and C07; assess sleep quality vs symptom impact.

solutions.solution_candidate: Enhance social connectedness and access to supportive networks through facilitated groups, buddy systems, and social prescribing where available.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Enhanced belonging and practical support reduce fear-avoidance and emotional distress, improving functioning.
solutions.measurement: Social connectedness scale; number of supportive contacts per week; avoidance days; mood/affect ratings.
solutions.safety_notes: Ensure respectful inclusion and privacy; monitor for over-reliance on social inputs.
solutions.mapping_query: Map S07 to C05 and C07; track changes in social engagement and symptom impact.

solutions.solution_candidate: Develop a structured plan for communicating with clinicians, including a patient-facing prompt list and collaborative interpretation of test results, to reduce perceived invalidation of reassurance.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Better communication reduces mistrust and reduces distress when medical results are normal but symptoms persist.
solutions.measurement: Patient satisfaction with reassurance; trust in clinician; perceived validation during medical encounters.
solutions.safety_notes: No safety concerns; avoid misinterpretation of medical advice; encourage questions.
solutions.mapping_query: Map S08 to C04 and C02; evaluate changes in reassurance acceptance and distress.

solutions.predictor_variable: Measurement-Based Care with Self-Monitoring Feedback Loop
solutions.solution_candidate: Implement regular self-monitoring of symptoms, triggers, and functioning with weekly feedback to tailor interventions. This creates data-driven adjustment of strategies and enhances engagement.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Frequent feedback and data-driven adjustments improve responsiveness of interventions and empower the patient.
solutions.measurement: Weekly symptom counts, 0–10 intensity, triggers, avoidance days; weekly feedback report; adherence to plan.
solutions.safety_notes: No direct safety concerns; ensure privacy and data security; escalate if new risk signals appear.
solutions.mapping_query: Map S09 to all primary criteria (C01-C07) via ongoing measurement and feedback.
